headline: All majors below 200-day lines with momentum fading; long-skewed positioning in alts absorbs selling while whales move BTC to exchanges.
paragraphs: The regime is unified in weakness. Every major asset in the tape, BTC through ADA, sits below its 200-day simple moving average. None have achieved a golden cross. Bitcoin trades at 64,562, down a quarter percent on the day and 48.3% below the 12-month high at 82,145. Ethereum is 61% from peak, Solana 69%, and the outlier ADA sits 83% below its high. The 90-day range for each asset shows the sprawl: Bitcoin between 58,550 and 82,145, Ethereum between 1,564 and 2,377. There are no islands of relative strength; the structure tilts lower.

Momentum offers a mixed signal that hints at wear rather than reversal. Bitcoin's RSI-14 stands at 54.5, Ethereum's at 61.3, both neutral to lightly elevated for their respective sell-offs. But Solana's RSI is 50.4 (truly neutral) and Doge's has rolled to 40.4, flashing a softer underbelly. MACD is above signal line for BTC, ETH, XRP, BNB, ADA, and Doge, but below for Solana only. That Solana sits alone on the wrong side of MACD while holding RSI at 50 is a divergence worth monitoring, though the sample size in equities would tell you a single negative outlier amid six positive readings is typically noise. Realized volatility tells the real story: ADA at 72%, Solana at 52.8%, and Ethereum at 47.3% all sit well above Bitcoin's 33.6%, suggesting the bet is fracturing and smaller-cap assets are experiencing more violent repricing.

Derivatives positioning reveals the tension. Bitcoin's perpetual funding on OKX is 0.0015% per 8 hours (1.7% annualized), cheap enough to carry a long. Open interest at 1.98 billion USD is substantial but not extreme given the asset's size. The long-short ratio at 1.24 means longs outnumber shorts by a quarter, a crowded setup, and in the last 24 hours liquidations hit 1,285 contracts worth 7.2 million USD on longs versus 2.3 million on shorts. Ethereum's funding is hotter at 0.0037% per 8 hours (4% annualized), and its liquidation tally is grimmer: 10.8 million USD in longs blown out against 1.76 million in shorts over 22 hours. The smaller alts show the real crowding. Doge's long-short ratio stands at 4.3, XRP at 2.74, SOL at 2.48. These are heavily long-skewed bets. XRP's funding sits at 0.01% per 8 hours (11% annualized), and Doge's at 0.0079% (8.6% annualized), both expensive to hold. When a small-cap perpetual trades at 8 to 11% annualized funding, the market is pricing in either imminent repricing or a consensus that longs will need to be flushed. The liquidation mix in these positions tells the same story: SOL's longs liquidated at 1.86 million USD while shorts took 77,000 USD out; Doge's longs liquidated 467,000 USD against 20,000 shorts. The tape is liquidating longs in the alts, not shorts.

Whale flows and stablecoin float carry a contradiction that underscores the moment. Over the last 24 hours, net whale Bitcoin movement was negative 40 million USD onto exchanges, with 105 million flowing into Coinbase Institutional and 65 million leaving it. That inflow to the institutional venue is worth watching: it suggests someone with size is moving BTC into a sale funnel, though a single day means nothing. More telling is the stablecoin float: 306 billion USD total, down 2.22% over 30 days, and stablecoin buying power (the net direction of USDC moves, primarily) is negative 210 million USD. Whales are moving stablecoins off exchanges. Simultaneously, they are moving Bitcoin onto exchanges. That is a sell bias, not a buy bias. The weekly whale net data softens the read a touch: the last four weeks show inflows of 534 million, 1.98 billion, 863 million, and an outflow of 210 million. The trend has rolled negative in the most recent week, which is the first pullback after substantial buying.

Sentiment is extreme fear at 25, a reading it has touched three times in the last 30 days (range 11 to 28). Extreme fear is typically a crowded short signal in traditional markets, which has taught traders to fade it; here, the tape shows long liquidations dominating, not short, and positioning in alts remains heavily long. The spot ETF bid remains present: Bitcoin ETFs took in 107.7 million USD on 15 July, Ethereum 53.9 million. That is not panic capitulation; it is steady accumulation. The contradiction between extreme fear sentiment, long liquidations in the derivative book, long-skewed alt positioning, and steady spot inflows into crypto ETFs is the session. The crowd feels fear, yet real money in spots is arriving and longs in alts are getting flushed. These are not the same bet.

Watch three conditions: first, whether Bitcoin's inflow to Coinbase Institutional resolves as a sale over the next 48 hours (a funded dump signals capitulation) or sits as a positioning move (a reaccumulation signal after this dip). Second, whether Solana's MACD flip into negative territory is joined by others, which would suggest the momentum positive reading across the board is genuine or a whipsaw into fresh weakness. Third, whether stablecoin float ticks above 308 billion or continues lower, because the current float of 306 billion with negative weekly whale stablecoin moves means dry powder is shrinking into a market that is hitting capitulation extremes.
